chunks.text: The week after I graduated from university, I set off alone for southern Europe.
A cheap airline ticket and a worn - out backpack were all I had.
The old quarter of Barcelona was a maze of bare stone walls and narrow lanes.
My footsteps echoed against the walls.
At every turn, a small craft shop or a folk music bar appeared.
Down at the harbor, a dozen fishing boats were lined up.
the scent of fresh bread and flavor of rich spice drifted in the air and stirred my appetite.
I took a train south and stayed at a white village estate.
The owners were a generous elderly couple who served me dinner.
The furniture was old but well kept.
On the last morning, I leaned on the balcony and looked out at the sea.
This time of leisure was exactly what I had been seeking on the journey.
The world is far more friendly, and far more broad, than I had ever imagined.
